### USCDI+ Quality Element Support Expectations

Systems are expected to support this profile as part of implementing USCDI+ Quality V2 in FHIR and meeting the conformance requirements of this guide.

The following elements are specifically relevant to USCDI+ Quality V2 data classes and elements. Systems are expected to support these elements as applicable to their role in US Quality Core data exchange. See the [Must Support](must-support.html) section of this guide for more information. These elements are tagged as (USCDI+ Quality) in the formal profile views.

<!-- START AUTOGENERATED KEY ELEMENTS -->
<b>USCDI+ Quality Elements:</b>
<ul>
<li>identifier: (USCDI+ Quality) Member ID and other identifiers</li>
<li>identifier:memberid: (USCDI+ Quality) Member ID</li>
<li>identifier:memberid.type: (USCDI+ Quality) Member Number identifier type</li>
<li>status: (USCDI+ Quality) active | cancelled | draft | entered-in-error</li>
<li>type: (USCDI+ Quality) Coverage category such as medical or accident</li>
<li>subscriberId: (USCDI+ Quality) ID assigned to the subscriber</li>
<li>beneficiary: (USCDI+ Quality) Plan beneficiary</li>
<li>relationship: (USCDI+ Quality) Beneficiary relationship to the subscriber</li>
<li>period: (USCDI+ Quality) Coverage start and end dates</li>
<li>payor: (USCDI+ Quality) Issuer of the policy</li>
<li>class: (USCDI+ Quality) Additional coverage classifications</li>
<li>class:group: (USCDI+ Quality) Group</li>
<li>class:group.value: (USCDI+ Quality) Group Identifier</li>
<li>class:group.name: (USCDI+ Quality) Human readable description of the type and value</li>
<li>class:plan: (USCDI+ Quality) Plan</li>
<li>class:plan.value: (USCDI+ Quality) Plan Number</li>
<li>class:plan.name: (USCDI+ Quality) Plan Name</li>
</ul>
<!-- END AUTOGENERATED KEY ELEMENTS -->

Because this profile is derived from US Core, systems are also expected to support Must Support requirements and (USCDI) tagged elements inherited from US Core. The [Formal Views](#profile) section also provides the formal summary, definitions, terminology requirements, and mandatory element requirements.
